name: LAMP-2A preservation improved aged mouse liver maintenance and function
summary: A liver-specific LAMP-2A model provides direct mammalian aging support for restoring lysosomal degradation capacity.
statement: Preventing the age-related loss of LAMP-2A and chaperone-mediated autophagy in mouse liver was associated with lower damaged-protein accumulation, better stress handling, and improved liver function in old animals.
time_horizon: Old-age liver maintenance after lifelong LAMP-2A preservation
caveats: The intervention is genetic and liver-specific.
The study supports tissue function and cellular maintenance rather than lifespan extension.
Translation to a systemic human intervention remains unresolved.
